verbatim_prompt: The RequestBar organism built in 009-request-bar (components/organisms/RequestBar.tsx + RequestBar.css) renders functionally correct but visually drifts from design/reference.html. Against the reference's FILLED state the bar is too cramped (height/padding/internal spacing undersized), the URL input's border-radius and focus border don't match, the method pill does not show the per-method colour, the Send button's active (non-empty-url) state is not the solid-primary + ⌘↵ affordance the reference shows, and Save/Share read as bare undersized icons rather than the reference's laid-out, labelled actions. Desired: RequestBar matches design/reference.html as a VISUAL reference with ZERO behaviour change — a pure styling pass (RequestBar.css + className edits in RequestBar.tsx) that leaves RequestSpec read/write, the onSend intent, the ⌘Enter/⌘S shortcuts, dirty/markClean, the empty-after-trim canSend guard, and per-tab isolation untouched, with all existing unit + CT tests staying green. design/reference.html is a VISUAL reference ONLY — do NOT pattern after, transcribe, or import any design-export markup/JSX or cruft (data-om-*, __OmT, tweaks-panel); rebuild fidelity with semantic classes bound to tokens.css and token values from design/tokens.json. Suspected cause: the drift is token-APPLICATION in RequestBar.css (classes bind missing or wrong token custom-properties, and the method pill is not consuming the 005 .method/.{METHOD} colour convention), NOT a logic defect. Treat that as a CLAIM TO DISPROVE: verify against the real component + token graph whether the cause is (a) token APPLICATION local to RequestBar.css; (b) a token GAP where tokens.css itself lacks the needed spacing/radius/method-colour custom-properties; or (c) a method-pill wiring issue where per-method colour depends on data-mstyle which per 009 AC-6 ONLY Shell writes. EXCLUDE from the drift: the empty-state appearance where Send is greyed because canSend is false on an empty URL, and any chrome from epics outside 009's scope. Scope: localised to RequestBar if the cause is pure application; feature-wide if it is a token gap. unchanged_behaviour: no change to RequestSpec read/write, the onSend intent, ⌘Enter/⌘S shortcut behaviour, dirty + markClean, the empty-after-trim Send guard, per-tab render isolation, and the Shell-as-sole-writer-of-data-mstyle invariant (009 AC-6) — RequestBar must still NOT write document.documentElement.dataset.mstyle.

symptom.value: Against design/reference.html's FILLED state, RequestBar drifts visually: bar too cramped (height/padding/internal spacing undersized); URL input border-radius + focus border wrong; method pill missing per-method colour; Send active (non-empty URL) not solid-primary + ⌘↵ affordance; Save/Share are bare undersized icons not laid-out labelled actions. Empty-state Send greying is EXCLUDED (correct canSend guard).

affected_area.value: RequestBar organism (src/renderer/src/components/organisms/RequestBar.tsx + co-located RequestBar.css) is definitely affected. tokens.css / design/tokens.json presumed correct but unverified — investigation must confirm whether the needed spacing/radius/method-colour custom-properties exist there or are a gap.

repro_or_current.value: Mount RequestBar in the app request pane (or its story/CT harness) and render it. Most drift (cramped height/padding/spacing, wrong URL-input radius, greyscale method pill, bare undersized Save/Share) is visible immediately at render WITHOUT typing. Only the Send active solid-primary + ⌘↵ affordance and the focus border require a non-empty URL / focus to observe. Compare against design/reference.html FILLED state.

desired.value: RequestBar visually matches design/reference.html in BOTH states (empty/not-filled AND filled): correct bar height/padding/internal spacing, matching URL-input border-radius + focus border, per-method coloured pill via the 005 .method/.{METHOD} class convention (NOT a data-mstyle write), Send active = solid-primary + ⌘↵ affordance, Save/Share laid-out + labelled. The ONE exclusion: Send greyed-disabled on an empty URL is correct canSend-guard behaviour, not a drift to fix. Fidelity rebuilt with semantic classes bound to tokens.css (values from design/tokens.json) — NOT by importing design-export markup/cruft (data-om-*, __OmT, tweaks-panel). Zero behaviour change; all unit + CT tests green.

unchanged_behavior.value: No change to: RequestSpec read/write; the onSend intent; ⌘Enter/⌘S shortcut behaviour; dirty + markClean; the empty-after-trim canSend Send guard; per-tab render isolation; the Shell-as-sole-writer-of-data-mstyle invariant (009 AC-6) — RequestBar must still NOT write document.documentElement.dataset.mstyle. All existing unit + CT tests stay green.

gaps.description: Confirm the exact reference geometry/treatment target values via a runtime design-auditor screenshot + computed-style diff against design/reference.html (filled state) before locking RequestBar.css values — diagnosis is code-confirmed but not yet runtime-verified.

Decide separately whether the shared stylesheet should expose un-prefixed per-verb default rules for contexts the shell has not initialised (CT/stories) — design-system-scope question, deferred from this localised pass.

intake_classifications.statement: RequestBar must match design/reference.html's FILLED state visually (height/padding/spacing, URL input radius+focus border, per-method pill colour, Send active solid-primary + ⌘↵ affordance, laid-out labelled Save/Share) with ZERO behaviour change — a pure styling pass over RequestBar.css + className edits in RequestBar.tsx, all unit+CT tests staying green.
intake_classifications.minimal_fix: Edit RequestBar.css (and RequestBar.tsx classNames only) so the filled-state classes bind the correct existing token custom-properties and the method pill uses the 005 .method/.{METHOD} class path; add tokens to tokens.css ONLY if a needed custom-property is proven absent. No JS/behaviour edits.

intake_classifications.statement: Suspected cause: the drift is token-APPLICATION in RequestBar.css (classes bind missing/wrong token custom-properties; method pill not consuming the 005 .method/.{METHOD} colour convention), NOT a logic defect — possibly (a) local application, (b) a tokens.css gap, or (c) a method-pill data-mstyle wiring issue.
